---
title: Instructor Notes
---

## Teaching Focus

The primary aim is to help learners make practical, defensible decisions about digital research workflows, not just memorize policy language.

## Delivery Guidance

- Prioritise scenario-based activities over long policy exposition.
- Encourage participants to map concepts to their own projects.
- Use short debrief loops after each task to consolidate learning.

## Common Sticking Points

- Confusion between governance policy and day-to-day workflow decisions.
- Overly generic DMP responses lacking operational detail.
- Limited understanding of role boundaries for data responsibility.

## Suggested Facilitation Moves

- Ask learners to justify tool/storage choices against risk level.
- Use “what would you do next week?” prompts to force concrete planning.
- Surface trade-offs explicitly (openness vs sensitivity, speed vs rigor).
